title: Calibration Workflow with cuvis.r
text: vignette demonstrates full calibration pipeline Cubert hyperspectral data: loading raw measurements, setting dark white references, reprocessing calibrated reflectance.

title: Calibration Workflow with cuvis.r
text: Cubert cameras capture raw sensor data must calibrated several stages: 1. Raw – unprocessed sensor readout 2. Dark subtract – dark current noise removed 3. Spectral radiance – calibrated physical units (W/m^2/sr/nm) 4. Reflectance – normalized white reference (values 0–1)

title: Calibration Workflow with cuvis.r
code: library(cuvis.r)  cuvis_init()  # Load the measurement session session <- cuvis_session("path/to/measurement.cu3s") mesu <- cuvis_get_measurement(session, 1)

title: Calibration Workflow with cuvis.r
text: processing context holds calibration state created session file. session already contains embedded references, loaded automatically:
code: ctx <- cuvis_processing_context(session, load_references = TRUE)  # Check which references were loaded cuvis_has_reference(ctx, "dark") cuvis_has_reference(ctx, "white")

title: Calibration Workflow with cuvis.r
text: session contain embedded references, load separate files:
code: # Load dark reference dark_session <- cuvis_session("path/to/dark.cu3s") dark_mesu <- cuvis_get_measurement(dark_session, 1) cuvis_set_reference(ctx, dark_mesu, "dark")  # Load white reference white_session <- cuvis_session("path/to/white.cu3s") white_mesu <- cuvis_get_measurement(white_session, 1) cuvis_set_reference(ctx, white_mesu, "white")

title: Calibration Workflow with cuvis.r
code: cuvis_reprocess(ctx, mesu, mode = "reflectance")  # Extract the calibrated cube cube <- cuvis_get_cube(mesu) dim(cube)  # Reflectance values should be roughly in [0, 1] range(cube, na.rm = TRUE)

title: Calibration Workflow with cuvis.r
code: # Get raw data first mesu_raw <- cuvis_get_measurement(session, 1) cuvis_reprocess(ctx, mesu_raw, mode = "raw") cube_raw <- cuvis_get_cube(mesu_raw)  # Compare a single pixel spectrum pixel_raw <- cube_raw[50, 50, ] pixel_ref <- cube[50, 50, ] wavelengths <- attr(cube, "wavelengths")  plot(wavelengths, pixel_raw, type = "l", col = "gray",      xlab = "Wavelength (nm)", ylab = "Value",      main = "Raw vs Reflectance") par(new = TRUE) plot(wavelengths, pixel_ref, type = "l", col = "blue",      axes = FALSE, xlab = "", ylab = "") axis(4) legend("topright", c("Raw", "Reflectance"),        col = c("gray", "blue"), lty = 1)

title: Calibration Workflow with cuvis.r
code: # Export as ENVI (compatible with terra, stars, hsdar, etc.) cuvis_export_envi(mesu, "output/calibrated/")  # Export as multi-channel TIFF cuvis_export_tiff(mesu, "output/tiff/",                   format = "MultiChannel", compression = "LZW")

title: Calibration Workflow with cuvis.r
Calibration Workflow with cuvis.r
text: hyperspectR package installed, uses cuvis.r backend:
code: library(hyperspectR)  # Reads .cu3s files using cuvis.r internally cube <- hs_read_cubert("measurement.cu3s")  # Full hyperspectR analysis pipeline hs_plot_clinical(cube)

title: Getting Started with cuvis.r

title: Getting Started with cuvis.r

title: Getting Started with cuvis.r
text: Every cuvis.r session begins cuvis_init() ends cuvis_shutdown():

title: Getting Started with cuvis.r
text: Cubert cameras store measurements .cu3s session files. Load one cuvis_session():

title: Getting Started with cuvis.r
text: Session files contain one measurements. Access index (1-based):

title: Getting Started with cuvis.r
code: md <- cuvis_get_metadata(mesu) md$name md$integration_time md$serial_number md$product_name

title: Getting Started with cuvis.r
text: hyperspectral data cube returned 3D R array [rows, cols, bands] wavelength metadata attached: can subset bands, compute means, use standard R array operations:
code: # First, process to generate the cube ctx <- cuvis_processing_context(session) cuvis_reprocess(ctx, mesu, mode = "raw")  cube <- cuvis_get_cube(mesu) dim(cube)                        # e.g., c(100, 100, 61) attr(cube, "wavelengths")        # wavelengths in nm # Mean reflectance per band band_means <- apply(cube, 3, mean, na.rm = TRUE)  # Extract a single band (e.g., band 30) single_band <- cube[, , 30] image(single_band, main = "Band 30")

title: Getting Started with cuvis.r
code: # ENVI format (widely supported by remote sensing tools) cuvis_export_envi(mesu, "output/envi/")  # Multi-channel TIFF cuvis_export_tiff(mesu, "output/tiff/")

title: Getting Started with cuvis.r
text: Portions package prepared assistance large language model tooling narrowly defined, non-authorial tasks: copyediting, prose smoothing, Markdown/LaTeX formatting, scaffolding boilerplate files (CI configs, build scripts), code refactoring. tools used Chat AI, LLM service KISSKI (GWDG), self-hosted Mistral Small (24B, Apache-2.0) run locally via Ollama ollamar R package — local inference , data sent third parties self-hosted model. scientific claims, methodological choices, analyses, interpretations, conclusions author’s . LLM-generated text incorporated without review revision, every reference verified DOI, arXiv ID, ISBN.

what: R Bindings for the Cubert CUVIS Hyperspectral SDK
title: R Bindings for the Cubert CUVIS Hyperspectral SDK
text: cuvis.r provides R bindings Cubert CUVIS SDK reading, calibrating, exporting hyperspectral data Cubert cameras. R equivalent cuvis.python.

title: R Bindings for the Cubert CUVIS Hyperspectral SDK
text: Install Cubert CUVIS SDK (>= 3.4.0) cloud.cubert-gmbh.de Set CUVIS_SDK environment variable install directory (Optional) Download sample data cloud.cubert-gmbh.de

title: R Bindings for the Cubert CUVIS Hyperspectral SDK
R Bindings for the Cubert CUVIS Hyperspectral SDK
code: library(cuvis.r)  cuvis_init()  # Load a session file session <- cuvis_session("path/to/measurement.cu3s") print(session)  # Extract the data cube mesu <- cuvis_get_measurement(session, 1) ctx <- cuvis_processing_context(session) cuvis_reprocess(ctx, mesu, mode = "raw") cube <- cuvis_get_cube(mesu) dim(cube)                        # [rows, cols, bands] attr(cube, "wavelengths")        # wavelengths in nm  # Calibrate to reflectance cuvis_reprocess(ctx, mesu, mode = "reflectance") ref_cube <- cuvis_get_cube(mesu)  # Export to ENVI cuvis_export_envi(mesu, "output/")  cuvis_shutdown()

title: R Bindings for the Cubert CUVIS Hyperspectral SDK
code: cuvis_init()  session <- cuvis_session("measurement.cu3s") mesu <- cuvis_get_measurement(session, 1)  # Load separate dark/white reference files dark_session <- cuvis_session("dark.cu3s") white_session <- cuvis_session("white.cu3s")  ctx <- cuvis_processing_context(session) cuvis_set_reference(ctx, cuvis_get_measurement(dark_session, 1), "dark") cuvis_set_reference(ctx, cuvis_get_measurement(white_session, 1), "white")  cuvis_reprocess(ctx, mesu, mode = "reflectance") ref_cube <- cuvis_get_cube(mesu)  cuvis_shutdown()

title: R Bindings for the Cubert CUVIS Hyperspectral SDK
text: cuvis.r designed /O backend hyperspectR:
code: library(hyperspectR)  # hyperspectR uses cuvis.r internally for .cu3s files cube <- hs_read_cubert("measurement.cu3s")

what: cuvis.r: R Bindings for the Cubert CUVIS Hyperspectral SDK — cuvis.r-package
title: cuvis.r: R Bindings for the Cubert CUVIS Hyperspectral SDK — cuvis.r-package
text: Provides R bindings Cubert CUVIS C SDK reading, processing, exporting hyperspectral imaging data Cubert snapshot cameras. Supports loading proprietary '.cu3s' session files, applying full calibration pipeline (dark subtraction, white reference normalization, spectral radiance, reflectance), exporting standard formats (ENVI, TIFF). Requires Cubert CUVIS SDK installed separately. package mirrors API 'cuvis.python' (https://github.com/cubert-hyperspectral/cuvis.python) R users.

title: cuvis.r: R Bindings for the Cubert CUVIS Hyperspectral SDK — cuvis.r-package
text: Maintainer: Raban Heller raban.heller@charite.de (ORCID)

title: CUVIS Processing Modes — cuvis_processing_modes
text: Raw (0): Raw sensor data, processing DarkSubtract (1): Dark current subtracted Reflectance (2): Normalized reflectance (0-1) SpectralRadiance (3): Calibrated physical radiance (W/m^2/sr/nm) Preview (5): Low-resolution preview

CUVIS Reference Types — cuvis_reference_types
text: object class integer length 5.

title: CUVIS Reference Types — cuvis_reference_types
text: Dark (0): Dark reference frame White (1): White reference frame WhiteDark (2): Dark reference white reference SpRad (3): Spectral radiance calibration Distance (4): Distance calibration

title: Reprocess a Measurement to a New Calibration Level — cuvis_reprocess
code: cuvis_reprocess(   ctx,   measurement,   mode = c("reflectance", "spectral_radiance", "dark_subtract", "raw", "preview"),   allow_recalib = FALSE )

title: Reprocess a Measurement to a New Calibration Level — cuvis_reprocess
text: ctx cuvis_processing_context references set. measurement cuvis_measurement reprocess. mode Character. Target processing mode: "reflectance" (default), "spectral_radiance", "dark_subtract", "raw", "preview". allow_recalib Logical. Allow recalibration needed. Default FALSE.

title: Reprocess a Measurement to a New Calibration Level — cuvis_reprocess
text: cuvis_measurement object, reprocessed place. Extract cube cuvis_get_cube().

title: cuvis.r (development version)
text: cuvis_get_measurement() now raises informative error unknown item_type instead cryptic “subscript bounds” error.

title: cuvis.r 0.1.0
text: Initial release. R bindings Cubert CUVIS C SDK via .Call() interface. Support loading .cu3s session files (cuvis_session()). Measurement extraction metadata access. 3D cube extraction R arrays wavelength attributes. Full calibration pipeline: dark subtract, spectral radiance, reflectance. Export ENVI, TIFF, .cu3s formats. Stub mode installation without SDK present. Configure script automatic SDK detection Linux, macOS, Windows.
